findings.detail: Mode detection can classify purely untracked current kit files as committed when the same paths existed in old history. `collectFacts` records `introducedBy` from `git log --diff-filter=A` for every present tool file regardless of `tracked` (around lines 193-205), and `buildPlan` decides `committed`/`mixed` from `introducedBy` alone (around lines 309-316). If a repo previously added and later deleted `scripts/req/**`, then a fresh uncommitted init will still find stale introduction commits and may print `git revert <old-sha>` instead of treating the current files as uncommitted. Gate commit inference on current `tracked` status, and add a regression where kit paths have historical add/delete commits before a new untracked install.

next_action: Use only currently tracked tool files when deriving scaffold commits/mode, add the historical add/delete + fresh untracked init regression, then rerun typecheck/tests/smoke.

observations.detail: The previous `rm -rf scripts/req` blocker is addressed in this revision: output now lists classified files individually and adds unknown-file tests for extra target-only files under `scripts/req/`.

`npm run typecheck` completed with exit 0. Focused Vitest could not start in this read-only sandbox because Vite attempted to write `vitest.config.ts.timestamp-*.mjs` under the workspace and hit EPERM.
